problem.statement: Partner couriers must bind to an authenticated captain identity through a short-lived one-time code without leaking tenant data or creating a second local fleet truth.
problem.currentWorkaround: The pre-closure implementation had partner and captain mutations but lacked complete operator readback, visible partner connection history, durable audited expiry, strict response schemas, and full same-commit PostgreSQL proof.

actors.role: Owns courier team records and one-time connection-code lifecycle for the authenticated store.
permittedActions: observe expiry redemption and disconnect outcomes

actors.role: Consumes a one-time code and owns the decision to disconnect each resulting store membership.
forbiddenActions: hold duplicate memberships in one store

actors.role: Reads a redacted operational snapshot for support, diagnostics, and audit.

actions: audit issue redeem revoke expire disconnect
prevent duplicate identity binding inside one store
allow all five JRN-030 lifecycle audit labels

outcome.statement: Partner, captain, and operator surfaces observe one versioned DSH fleet truth while code material remains secret and every lifecycle mutation is scoped, audited, notified, and recoverable.
outcome.baselineState: Partner and captain flows existed but operator readback, partner-visible connection state, and several lifecycle integrity controls were incomplete at the current head.
outcome.targetState: All required surfaces use the same DSH state; all lifecycle transitions are scoped, versioned, audited, and notified; typed contracts and PostgreSQL proof run on one commit.
outcome.observationWindow: Every JRN-030 change, runtime smoke, pull-request review, and release review.

criteria: Only an authenticated partner for the owned store can issue list or revoke a code.
Partner surfaces visibly render pending redeemed revoked and expired connection states.
Only an authenticated captain can redeem a valid pending code or disconnect an owned membership.
A captain may hold memberships in multiple stores but cannot hold duplicate memberships inside one store.
Plaintext codes are returned once and never persisted.
Expired codes become durably expired, create an audit action and partner notification, and cannot be redeemed.
Inactive stores and ineligible courier records fail closed.
Optimistic versions protect revoke redeem and disconnect transitions.
Disconnect pauses the member, records captain_disconnected lifecycle, revokes the redeemed connection, and notifies both actors.
The database audit guard permits issue redeem revoke expire and disconnect lifecycle labels and preserves same-status security events.
Control panel readback is redacted and requires partners.read or operator authority.
OpenAPI responses are typed and do not expose code hashes or plaintext outside issuance.
Targeted Go TypeScript contract binding Product Truth and PostgreSQL checks pass on the same commit.
failureStates: duplicate captain membership inside one store
unaudited issue redeem revoke expiry or disconnect
closure evidence references an older commit
requiredChecks: JRN-030 PostgreSQL lifecycle and tenant-isolation test
DSH partner-fleet OpenAPI parse and schema verification
visual evidence and independent product QA security and release review

business: DSH owns partner fleet membership truth.
Identity authenticates actors while DSH authorizes store and team scope.
Every surface reads the same persisted membership and connection lifecycle.
Every mutation is versioned tenant-scoped audited and notified.
Captain identity uniqueness is scoped to one store so governed multi-store membership remains possible.
Fleet lifecycle audit facts remain durable even when a transition does not change team-member status.
negative: no duplicate captain membership inside one store
no expired or revoked code reuse
no suppressed same-status fleet lifecycle audit
no missing partner or captain notification for successful binding and disconnect
no operator mutation through the readback surface
no final production closure without independent approvals runtime evidence and visual evidence

execution.appetite: Close all JRN-030 technical slices on GitHub Remote without granting independent product QA security release or production approval.
fixedConstraints: GitHub Remote sambassam is the only execution truth
DSH remains the sole fleet membership owner
